4-judge ensemble on red_5k_v2 (1500 grit_v2 + 1500 cc3m + 700 vg + 300 oi + 300 pixmo)
Each judged sample shows the 4-judge verdict chips + brief reasons. Categorized by agreement: ALL-AGREE-YES = definite false-reject (Molmo correct, GT too restrictive); ALL-AGREE-NO = real hard example (Molmo wrong); tied = ambiguous.
Judges done: GPT-5=5000/4300, Qwen3-VL-32B=5000/4300, InternVL3-78B=5000/4300, GLM-4.5V=5000/4300 · 3-judge intersection=5000 · 4-judge intersection=5000
datasetnall_mask_wrmaj_mask_wrall_yesmaj_yestiedmaj_noall_no
cc3m1500278 (19%)345 (23%)214 (14%)254 (17%)237 (16%)125 (8%)47 (3%)
grit_v21500514 (34%)383 (26%)239 (16%)197 (13%)91 (6%)53 (4%)23 (2%)
openimages700279 (40%)178 (25%)49 (7%)115 (16%)37 (5%)30 (4%)12 (2%)
pixmo60030 (5%)89 (15%)96 (16%)281 (47%)58 (10%)27 (4%)19 (3%)
vg700373 (53%)154 (22%)74 (11%)45 (6%)31 (4%)16 (2%)7 (1%)
🚫 ALL agree mask_wrong (DROP — GT broken consensus) (1474 total · showing 15)
vg / 00000__vg_0004659__0705f4 · kind=bbox
the rug on the ground
F1=0.67P=0.50R=1.00n_pred=2 n_gt=1
GT: [608,331,718,673]
PRED: (261,254), (261,356)
pred_raw: <points coords="1 1 510 662 2 510 927">rug on the ground.</points>
GPT-5:mask: mask_wrongmolmo: no
Qwen:mask: mask_wrongmolmo: no
InternVL:mask: mask_wrongmolmo: yes
GLM:mask: mask_wrongmolmo: yes
GPT-5: The phrase targets rugs on the floor. Several rugs are visible on the ground, but the yellow mask highlights a chair area, not a rug, so the GT is wrong/under-annotated. The two red dots are placed on the tiled floor areas, not on any rug, so they do not point to the target.
Qwen: Stage 1: The yellow mask highlights a transparent plastic chair, not a rug on the ground. The phrase 'the rug on the ground' clearly refers to the large red patterned rug visible in the foreground, which is not covered by the yellow mask. The mask is therefore incorrect. Stage 2: The two red dots are placed on the tiled floor, not on the rug. They do not point to any instance of 'the rug on the ground', so the model's predictions are incorrect.
InternVL: Stage 1: The yellow annotation is on a chair, not the rug. Stage 2: The red dots are correctly pointing at the rug.
GLM: Stage 1: The yellow annotation is on a chair, not the rug, so it's mask_wrong. Stage 2: Both red dots are on the rug, which matches the phrase, so molmo_verdict is yes.
grit_v2 / coyo_0_snappy_00000__000007940__78386e · kind=bbox
People
F1=0.22P=0.14R=0.50n_pred=14 n_gt=4
GT: [298,283,473,319], [297,180,454,215], [299,370,442,403], [305,234,482,269]
PRED: (148,275), (170,275), (196,266), (226,279), (263,279), (335,294), (344,227), (370,294) …+6
pred_raw: <points coords="1 1 246 612 2 283 612 3 326 592 4 376 619 5 438 619 6 558 654 7 573 504 8 616 654 9 676 642 10 763 582 11 796 619 12 858 612 13 913 529 14 933 512">People</points>
GPT-5:mask: mask_wrongmolmo: yes
Qwen:mask: mask_wrongmolmo: yes
InternVL:mask: mask_wrongmolmo: yes
GLM:mask: mask_wrongmolmo: yes
GPT-5: Stage 1: The yellow masks are horizontal bars that only partially cover a few central people and also include empty road area; they miss many visible people along the left and right, so the GT is under- and mis-annotated for “People.” Stage 2: The red dots each land on individuals standing or sitting outside the venue across the scene; they consistently point to people and cover many of them, so the model’s dots are appropriate for the phrase.
Qwen: Stage 1: The yellow mask covers multiple people, but it is drawn as a large rectangular region that includes parts of the building facade, railings, and pavement — not just the people. It does not accurately segment the individuals; instead, it over-annotates by including non-person areas. The mask also appears to cover more than 4 instances (the stated N_GT=4), suggesting misalignment with the actual count of people. Thus, the mask is wrong. Stage 2: The red dots are placed on various individuals standing along the sidewalk and near the bar entrance. All 14 dots appear to be on people — no dots are on non-person objects. Although there are many people visible, the model has placed dots on multiple individuals, and the count (14) is reasonable given the number of visible people. Therefore, the red dots correctly point at 'People'.
InternVL: Stage 1: The yellow mask covers only a few people and misses many others, making it under-annotated. Stage 2: The red dots point at people, and the count roughly matches the visible instances.
GLM: Stage 1: The yellow annotation is over-annotated, covering large areas that include non-people elements (e.g., building facades, street) and not accurately marking individual people. Stage 2: All red dots point to actual people in the image, and the count (14) roughly matches the visible number of people.

pred_raw: <points coords="1 1 186 448">wall is off white</points>
GPT-5:mask: mask_wrongmolmo: no
Qwen:mask: mask_wrongmolmo: no
InternVL:mask: mask_wrongmolmo: yes
GLM:mask: mask_wrongmolmo: no
GPT-5: The yellow regions highlight parts of the ceiling/window header area, not the off-white walls visible on the left and right. The single red dot is on the left wall, which matches the phrase, but many visible off-white wall areas (large left wall and shoji-style panels on right) are not indicated, so coverage/count is insufficient.
Qwen: Stage 1: The yellow mask covers the ceiling beams and part of the upper wall, but the phrase 'wall is off white' refers to the wall surfaces. The mask incorrectly includes the ceiling and does not accurately represent the off-white walls — especially the large wall on the left and the shoji screen walls, which are off-white. The mask is misaligned and over-annotates non-wall areas. Stage 2: The single red dot is placed on the left wall, which is off-white, so it correctly points to an instance. However, since there are two visible off-white walls (left wall and shoji screens), and only one dot is placed, the model misses one instance. Given N_GT=2, the model under-predicts, so verdict is 'no'.
InternVL: Stage 1: The yellow region is over-annotated, covering the ceiling and not just the off-white wall. Stage 2: The red dot correctly points to the off-white wall.
GLM: Stage 1: The yellow annotation incorrectly marks the ceiling beams instead of the off-white walls. Stage 2: The single red dot points at a wall, but the model missed most visible wall areas and the count doesn't match the actual number of wall sections.

F1=0.00P=0.00R=0.00n_pred=1 n_gt=1
GT: [316,55,536,368]
PRED: (238,381)
pred_raw: <points coords="1 1 396 762">A large bowl</points>
GPT-5:mask: mask_wrongmolmo: yes
Qwen:mask: mask_wrongmolmo: no
InternVL:mask: mask_wrongmolmo: no
GLM:mask: mask_wrongmolmo: yes
GPT-5: Stage 1: The yellow mask covers the right side of the fruit (bananas, etc.) and misses the large blue bowl; it marks the wrong object. Stage 2: The single red dot is positioned on the large blue bowl, correctly indicating the target.
Qwen: Stage 1: The yellow mask covers a banana and part of the watermelon, not the large bowl. The bowl is the blue container holding the fruit, which is not covered by the yellow mask. Thus, the mask is incorrect. Stage 2: The single red dot is placed on the blue bowl, which is the correct object for 'A large bowl'. However, since the model only predicted 1 point and there is only 1 bowl, and the point is correctly placed, the verdict should be 'yes'. But wait — the red dot is actually placed on the side of the bowl, near the bottom, which is still part of the bowl. So it is correctly pointing at the bowl. Therefore, molmo_verdict should be 'yes'. Correction: I misjudged the red dot's position. It is indeed on the bowl. So: - mask_wrong: yellow mask is on banana/watermelon, not the bowl. - molmo_yes: red dot is correctly on the bowl. Final: mask_wrong, molmo_yes.
InternVL: Stage 1: The yellow region is incorrectly placed over the banana and not covering the bowl. Stage 2: The red dot is pointing at a grape, not the bowl.
GLM: Stage 1: The yellow annotation does not cover the large bowl (it covers the banana and empty space instead). Stage 2: The single red dot is positioned on the large bowl, so it correctly points at an instance of the phrase.

pred_raw: <points coords="1 1 118 346 2 654 346">white clouds in the blue sky.</points>
GPT-5:mask: mask_wrongmolmo: no
Qwen:mask: mask_wrongmolmo: no
InternVL:mask: mask_wrongmolmo: yes
GLM:mask: mask_wrongmolmo: yes
GPT-5: Stage 1: The yellow mask covers most of the left half of the image including monument, buildings, people, and sky. It is not restricted to the white clouds in the blue sky, so it is over-annotated and incorrect. Stage 2: One red dot is on clouds/sky (left side), but the other dot is on a building on the right, not on clouds. Therefore the red dots do not correctly point only at the white clouds in the blue sky.
Qwen: Stage 1: The yellow mask covers the entire left half of the image, including the obelisk, buildings, and sky. However, it does not specifically isolate 'the white clouds in the blue sky' — it includes non-cloud elements and fails to distinguish the clouds from the sky or other objects. The mask is overly broad and not focused on the phrase. Stage 2: The two red dots are placed in the sky area, but they are not located on the white clouds — they appear to be on the blue sky background. Since the phrase specifically refers to 'white clouds', and the dots are not on clouds, the model's predictions are incorrect.
InternVL: Stage 1: The yellow annotation is incorrectly placed on the monument, not on the clouds in the sky. Stage 2: The red dots are correctly pointing at the clouds in the sky.
GLM: Stage 1: The yellow annotation covers the entire left image (including the obelisk and buildings), which is not limited to the white clouds in the blue sky. It is over-annotated and includes unrelated objects, so mask_wrong. Stage 2: The two red dots are positioned in the sky area where white clouds are visible. Both dots point at cloud regions, and the count (2) roughly matches the visible cloud instances, so yes.

GPT-5: The phrase asks for black thigh high boots, but the person is wearing gray thigh-high boots. The yellow masks mark the gray boots and also extend to surrounding areas; they do not correspond to black boots. The red dots also point to the gray boots, not to black thigh-high boots mentioned in the phrase.
Qwen: Stage 1: The yellow mask highlights a staircase and part of the woman's dress, not the boots. The phrase is 'black thigh high boots', but the mask does not cover any boots — it covers unrelated objects. Therefore, the mask is wrong. Stage 2: The two red dots are placed on the woman's gray thigh-high boots. Although the boots are gray, not black, the phrase 'black thigh high boots' is likely intended to refer to these boots (possibly due to lighting or color description error). The dots correctly point to the boots, and there are two visible boots, matching N_PRED=2. Thus, the model's points are accurate for the visible instances, even if the color description is slightly off. So, verdict is 'yes'.
InternVL: Stage 1: The yellow annotation is over-annotated, covering the stairs instead of the boots. Stage 2: The red dots are pointing at the gray boots, not the black thigh high boots.
GLM: Stage 1: The yellow annotation incorrectly covers stairs and a wall instead of the black thigh high boots. Stage 2: Both red dots accurately point to the black thigh high boots, matching the visible instances.

The image captures a breathtaking view of the south coast, where the landscape drops steeply into a serene body of water.
F1=0.00P=0.00R=0.00n_pred=1 n_gt=1
GT: (319,217)
PRED: (319,398)
pred_raw: <points coords="1 1 498 890">The image captures a breathtaking view of the south coast, where the landscape drops steeply into a serene body of water.</points>
GPT-5:mask: mask_wrongmolmo: no
Qwen:mask: mask_wrongmolmo: no
InternVL:mask: mask_wrongmolmo: no
GLM:mask: mask_wrongmolmo: no
GPT-5: The phrase refers to a broad coastal landscape with steep drop into water. The yellow annotation is just a small dot on the hillside, not covering the coastal scene or coastline; thus it's not an appropriate mask. The red dot is placed over the water surface, which doesn’t specifically indicate the described coastal landscape either, so it does not correctly point to the phrase.
Qwen: Stage 1: The yellow mask is a single small dot on a rocky slope, which does not represent the entire described scene — a breathtaking view of the south coast with steep landscape dropping into water. The phrase describes a broad landscape, not a single point. The mask is clearly wrong as it fails to cover any meaningful part of the described scene. Stage 2: The single red dot is placed on the water near the shore, which is part of the described 'serene body of water'. However, the phrase describes the entire view including the steep landscape and coast, not just the water. Since the model only placed one dot and it's on the water, it misses the key elements of the landscape and coast. Thus, it does not correctly point to the full phrase meaning.
InternVL: Stage 1: The yellow annotation does not accurately cover the entire landscape that drops into the water, missing significant parts of the coast. Stage 2: The red dot does not point to the landscape or the body of water, but rather to a different area.
GLM: Stage 1: The yellow annotation is a single dot on the hillside, which does not accurately represent the entire landscape description (coast, steep drop, water) and is not a valid mask for the phrase. Stage 2: The red dot is in the water, which is not the main subject of the phrase describing the landscape; it fails to point at the relevant landscape elements.

GT: [383,416,490,577], [222,418,322,577], [359,608,470,694], [157,618,304,694], [301,741,434,812], [0,880,159,998], [217,889,390,998]
PRED: (52,385), (99,454), (108,385), (124,454), (170,413), (277,464), (278,320), (286,464) …+15
pred_raw: <points coords="1 1 068 376 2 129 443 3 140 376 4 161 443 5 221 403 6 361 453 7 362 313 8 372 453 9 374 313 10 451 438 11 462 278 12 529 278 13 532 438 14 632 428 15 634 253 16 642 573 17 674 243 18 6
GPT-5:mask: mask_wrongmolmo: yes
Qwen:mask: mask_wrongmolmo: no
InternVL:mask: mask_wrongmolmo: yes
GLM:mask: mask_wrongmolmo: yes
GPT-5: Stage 1: The yellow annotations mostly cover planter boxes, grass, and other yard items rather than windows; they miss almost all actual windows on the houses. Stage 2: The red dots are placed on the window panes of the house(s) and generally correspond to windows with appropriate count coverage, so they correctly point at the phrase 'Window'.
Qwen: Stage 1: The yellow masks cover multiple objects, including windows, but also include non-window elements such as garden beds, grass patches, and parts of the house siding. The masks do not exclusively or accurately represent windows — for example, the large yellow rectangles in the garden area clearly cover planters and soil, not windows. The count of 7 GT instances is inconsistent with the visual content, as many of the masked regions are not windows. Thus, the mask is over-annotated and includes irrelevant objects. Stage 2: The red dots are placed on many windows, but also on non-window areas — for example, several red dots are on the house siding, on the ground near the foundation, and on the garden beds. Some dots are even on the windows of the neighboring house, which may not be part of the intended target. The model has clearly misidentified many non-window regions as windows, and the count of 23 is far higher than the actual number of visible windows (approximately 10–12 total
InternVL: Stage 1: The yellow mask does not accurately cover all the windows and seems to be over-annotated, covering areas that are not windows. Stage 2: The red dots correctly point to the windows, matching the visible instances.
GLM: Stage 1: The yellow annotations do not accurately mark windows; they cover unrelated objects like planters and grass. Stage 2: The red dots correctly point to windows, with counts roughly matching visible instances.

Thus, the prediction is incomplete and incorrect.

GLM: Stage 1: The yellow annotation accurately covers the trailer with seedlings and the seated man, matching the phrase's description without over/under-annotation. Stage 2: The single red dot points to the man on the trailer, which is part of the described scene, so it correctly identifies an instance of the phrase.
